reasoning: The FRED statistical tool estimates a 33% chance of the OAS increasing by March 22 from its current level of 0.93. The spread spiked sharply in early March (from 0.82 on March 4 to 0.93 on March 13). Such rapid widenings often see short-term pullbacks or stabilization unless a major systemic shock occurs. Although an FOMC meeting is scheduled for March 17-18, which introduces volatility that could push spreads higher, the general expectation in expert reports is that corporate fundamentals remain solid and spreads will remain largely range-bound in the medium term. Historically, around this time in March 2025, the spread peaked at 0.93 and slightly decreased to 0.92 by March 21. Given the mean-reverting tendency after a sharp 10-basis-point weekly spike and the tool's 33% anchor, I assign a 35% probability of a further increase by March 22.
